banner.title: Gemini Omni API Access Guide
banner.description: A practical guide for teams searching for Gemini Omni API access: what you can do inside DreamOmni today, when to use official Google Veo API paths, and how to plan credits, prompts, and production workflows without assuming unlimited free video generation.

tabs.title: Try a Gemini Omni-Style Prompt
tabs.subTitle: Prompt
tabs.placeholder: Describe the video you want to generate, including subject, camera motion, scene, style, and audio direction...

tabs.title: Use an Image Reference
tabs.subTitle: Image
tabs.placeholder: Upload a reference image to guide subject, composition, or product appearance
tabs.tips: Upload JPG, PNG, WEBP, or GIF images up to 20MB.

details.title: Use DreamOmni for Browser-Based Generation
details.description: DreamOmni is built for creators who want an online workflow first: sign in, submit text or image-to-video prompts, track generation history, and download finished assets. This is the fastest path when you need output without maintaining API credentials, queue workers, storage callbacks, or provider billing logic.

cells.content: Create videos from prompts or images without developer setup
Use DreamOmni text-to-video or image-to-video in the browser
Generate, review, refine prompts, then document the workflow
Need official programmable access to Google video models
Review the current Gemini API or Vertex AI Veo documentation before building

details.title: Plan Official API Work Separately
details.description: Official Google video API access changes over time and is billed separately from DreamOmni. Public Google docs currently list Veo 3.1 in paid Gemini API and Vertex AI paths, with per-second pricing rather than a permanent free tier. Treat official API integration as a developer project with budget controls, retries, moderation, storage, and usage monitoring.

cells.content: Google Cloud or Gemini API account, billing enabled, and secure server-side key handling
Per-second video pricing, usage caps, and clear refund or retry policy
Job queue, webhook/status polling, asset storage, and user-visible progress states

details.title: When an API Is Not the Right First Step
details.description: If your team is still testing prompts, brand rules, model quality, or campaign formats, a manual web workflow often teaches you more than an early API integration. Use DreamOmni to discover repeatable prompt patterns, then automate only the pieces that are stable enough to justify engineering work.

cells.content: Use the web generator and save winning prompts
Create templates, naming rules, and review checkpoints
Evaluate official APIs or managed provider APIs with cost monitoring

usageGuide.title: How to Start Without a Public Gemini Omni API
usageGuide.description: Use the web workflow first, then graduate to API planning only after your creative process is repeatable.

steps.title: Create with the browser tool
steps.description: Start with text-to-video or image-to-video and capture the prompts, references, durations, and settings that work.

steps.title: Document repeatable patterns
steps.description: Turn successful prompts into reusable briefs with scene, camera, subject, and brand constraints.

steps.title: Evaluate official APIs
steps.description: For automation, review current Google Gemini API or Vertex AI video docs and estimate per-second cost before implementation.

faq.name: faq
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Gemini Omni API Questions
faq.description: Practical answers for developers and teams evaluating Gemini Omni API access.

items.title: Does DreamOmni provide a public developer API?
items.description: DreamOmni is primarily a browser-based AI video generator. If you need official Google video API access, review the current Gemini API or Vertex AI documentation and pricing before building.

items.title: Can I use DreamOmni before building an API integration?
items.description: Yes. It is often smarter to validate prompts, references, and expected output quality in the web app before committing engineering time to automation.

items.title: Is official Veo 3.1 API access free?
items.description: Google public docs currently list Veo 3.1 on the paid tier for Gemini API, with video priced per second. Always check the official docs before budgeting.

items.title: Is DreamOmni affiliated with Google?
items.description: No. DreamOmni.ai is an independent product and is not affiliated with Google, Gemini, or Google DeepMind.

cta.title: Test the Workflow Before You Build the API
cta.description: Create a prompt-driven video in DreamOmni, then decide whether automation is worth the engineering work.
